title: The Powerhouse
description: The Powerhouse — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Powerhouse" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.description: "To"

memes.label: "Preface by the Editor"
memes.description: "We were at Glenaicill—six of us—for the duck-shooting, when Leithen told us this story."

memes.label: "The Powerhouse"
memes.description: "This section contains the following chapters:"

relations.label: I: Beginning of the Wild-Goose Chase
II: I First Hear of Mr. Andrew Lumley
III: Tells of a Midsummer Night
IV: I Follow the Trail of the Super-Butler
VI: The Restaurant in Antioch Street
IX: Return of the Wild Geese

memes.label: "Academy of Science"
memes.description: "A generic reference to scientific institutions, used by Andrew Lumley in his critique of conventional science in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Andrew Lumley
memes.description: The enigmatic intellectual whose dinner-table philosophy introduces the concept of the Powerhouse — a man of "light wild eyes" whose true relationship to the conspiracy he describes is the novel's central mystery

memes.label: Antioch Street
memes.description: A discreet side street near Tottenham Court Road containing the restaurant Rapaccini's — the Powerhouse's concealed operational base, where Leithen is lured into a trap in ch06

memes.label: "Marble Arch Station"
memes.description: "A London Underground station near Marble Arch, used by Leithen to shake off pursuit in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Bayswater Road"
memes.description: "The road bordering Hyde Park where Leithen emerges after escaping through the picture-shop in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Belgrave Square
memes.description: Location of the Embassy where Leithen's ally Felix works, serving as a safe haven during the Powerhouse crisis.

memes.label: "Berkeley Square"
memes.description: "A garden square in Mayfair, London, in the world of *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Bill Docker
memes.description: A criminal defendant defended by Leithen; later reappears in a brawl scene at the restaurant in The Powerhouse.

memes.label: Blackheath
memes.description: South London neighbourhood housing Julius Pavia's villa and Andrew Lumley's secondary residence, key to Leithen's investigation.

memes.label: Bokhara
memes.description: Central Asian destination of Charles Pitt-Heron's flight and the focal point of the wild-goose chase in The Powerhouse.

memes.label: "Bond Street"
memes.description: "Bond Street -- the fashionable London shopping street, referenced when describing Andrew Lumley's lavish art-filled home in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Boundary Commission"
memes.description: "An international commission for demarcating colonial frontiers, referenced in a backstory told by a club acquaintance in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "British Government"
memes.description: "The government of the United Kingdom, which forms part of the 'machine' of civilisation that the Powerhouse conspirators seek to subvert."

memes.label: "British Museum"
memes.description: The national museum of the United Kingdom, invoked in The Powerhouse as part of Andrew Lumley's cultural camouflage and the institutional Establishment whose acquisitions he privately rivals

relations.label: Chapter III: Tells of a Midsummer Night

memes.label: "Cannon Street"
memes.description: "A street in the City of London, home to a bucket-shop whose clerk provides a clue in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Capital and Labour"
memes.description: "The ideological framework through which the Labour MP Chapman interprets the Powerhouse conspiracy in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Carlton Hotel"
memes.description: "A prestigious London hotel, a haunt of the wealthy and powerful, mentioned as a dream landscape in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Central Asia
memes.description: The remote Central Asian wilderness that serves as the novel's offstage theatre — the destination of Charles Pitt-Heron's flight and the arena of the parallel pursuit across Turkestan in *The Powerhouse*

memes.description: "Beginning of the Wild-Goose Chase"

memes.description: "I First Hear of Mr."

memes.label: Chance
memes.description: The role of accident and contingency in the plot — Leithen's involvement begins by chance, crucial turns depend on random encounters, and chance itself emerges as civilisation's secret ally against the Powerhouse's attempt to eliminate contingency through superior knowledge and organisation

memes.label: "Chancery Lane"
memes.description: "The historic legal thoroughfare in Holborn, London, near which Chancery Lane Station was situated."

memes.label: Chapman
memes.description: Labour Member of Parliament and political ally of Tommy Deloraine, who provides intelligence on the Powerhouse's labour connections.

memes.label: Characters
memes.description: Index of all characters appearing in The Powerhouse, the 1916 novel by Edward Phillips Oppenheim

memes.label: Charles Pitt-Heron
memes.description: Leithen's old Oxford friend whose sudden and mysterious disappearance — abandoning his wife Ethel and fleeing to Bokhara — sets the entire plot in motion, his connection to the Powerhouse and Julius Pavia revealing the conspiracy's financial dimension

memes.label: Civilisation
memes.description: The central thematic concern of the novel — civilisation as a fragile "conspiracy" of comfortable people, a thin veneer held together by unspoken compacts rather than force

memes.label: Concepts
memes.description: Index of all concepts and themes explored in The Powerhouse

memes.label: "Connaught Mews"
memes.description: "A mews lane in the Edgeware Road district through which Leithen escapes his pursuers in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Conspiracy
memes.description: Lumley's provocative redefinition of civilisation itself as a "conspiracy" — a secret agreement among the comfortable to maintain their position — and the literal conspiracy of the Powerhouse that mirrors it

memes.label: "Constitution Hill"
memes.description: "The road along the edge of Green Park and Buckingham Palace Gardens, where Leithen walks after his confrontation with Lumley in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Court of Appeal"
memes.description: "The appellate court in England and Wales, referenced in John Buchan's *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Criminal Investigation Department"
memes.description: "The detective branch of London's Metropolitan Police, led in the novel by Leithen's acquaintance Macgillivray."

memes.label: Danger
memes.description: The objective condition of threat that drives the thriller plot — distinct from the subjective experience of fear, danger is the reality of the situation whether or not the protagonist fully perceives it

memes.label: "Devonshire House"
memes.description: "The grand Piccadilly mansion marking the point where Leithen re-enters the West End after his confrontation with Lumley in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Down Street
memes.description: Sir Edward Leithen's London flat in Down Street, Mayfair — his sanctuary and base of operations during the Powerhouse affair.

memes.label: "East Claygate"
memes.description: "A fictional parliamentary constituency whose member provides comic relief during a tense Commons scene in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: East India Merchant
memes.description: The commercial background of Julius Pavia, a retired East India merchant whose wealth and connections intersect with the Powerhouse conspiracy.

memes.label: "Edgeley Street"
memes.description: "A side street near Edgware Road, part of Leithen's escape route after eluding the fake chauffeur in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Edgware Road"
memes.description: "A major arterial road in northwest London where Leithen makes a daring escape from his would-be captors in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Sir Edward Leithen
memes.description: The narrator and protagonist — a barrister and MP who is drawn into investigating the disappearance of his friend Charles Pitt-Heron, only to discover the existence of the Powerhouse

memes.label: "England"
memes.description: "England as the setting and frame of reference in *The Powerhouse* by John Buchan."

memes.label: Ethel Pitt-Heron
memes.description: Wife of Charles Pitt-Heron and cousin of Tommy Deloraine, whose distress over her husband's disappearance drives the novel's opening.

memes.label: Events
memes.description: Index of all significant narrative events in The Powerhouse

memes.label: "Extra-social Intelligence"
memes.description: "Andrew Lumley's term for the exceptional intellects operating outside the conventions of society, the philosophical foundation of the Powerhouse conspiracy."

memes.label: "Farnham"
memes.description: "Farnham, Surrey -- destination near which Leithen's car breaks down in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: The psychological experience of being hunted by an invisible enemy — Leithen's fear is not the melodramatic terror of Gothic fiction but the rational, controlled dread of a man who understands exactly how vulnerable he is

memes.label: Monsieur Felix
memes.description: A diplomat at an unnamed Embassy and close friend of Sir Edward Leithen, who provides crucial intelligence support throughout the Powerhouse investigation.

memes.label: "Fountain Court"
memes.description: "Leithen's barristers' chambers in the Temple, the professional home from which he conducts his investigation in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Mr. Geelvrai
memes.description: Manager of a restaurant where private rooms are engaged, mentioned in The Powerhouse.

memes.label: "General Bertelin"
memes.description: "A French general associated with the Russian Embassy who is referenced in connection with the sanctuary scene, representing the military-diplomatic nexus within which the novel's action unfolds."

memes.label: Gentleman
memes.description: The Edwardian social code that governs Leithen's behaviour — a framework of restraint, amateur competence, and unspoken obligation that both enables and constrains the protagonist

memes.label: "Government Whip"
memes.description: "The parliamentary figure referenced in the opening scene — representing the mundane political world Leithen occupies before the Powerhouse draws him in."

memes.label: "Green Park"
memes.description: "Green Park -- the Royal Park in central London where Leithen takes refuge during his flight from the Powerhouse's agents in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Grosvenor Gate"
memes.description: "A gate into Hyde Park where Leithen pauses among well-dressed loungers during his flight in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Grosvenor Place
memes.description: London street where Andrew Lumley confronts Leithen and later where Leithen is pursued by the Powerhouse network.

memes.label: "Hamilton Place"
memes.description: "A short street off Park Lane where Leithen senses he is being watched during his desperate journey to the Embassy in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Hampshire"
memes.description: "Hampshire -- the English county where Andrew Lumley maintains a country house and where Leithen's chance encounter with him occurs in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Hertford Street"
memes.description: "A Mayfair street where Leithen notices one of Lumley's watchers crossing the opening in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Hewins"
memes.description: "The imperturbable butler at the Russian Embassy in London who provides Leithen with safe passage to Felix during a critical moment in the pursuit by Lumley's agents."

memes.label: "High Ashes"
memes.description: "Andrew Lumley's country house in Hampshire — the setting for the Midsummer Night dinner where he reveals the philosophy of the Powerhouse."

memes.label: His Excellency
memes.description: The unnamed Ambassador at the Russian Embassy in Belgrave Square who provides sanctuary to Leithen during his flight from Lumley's agents.

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: The recognition and prestige sought by scientific discoverers, contrasted against Lumley's nihilistic rejection of conventional rewards in *The Powerhouse*

memes.label: "House of Commons"
memes.description: "The lower house of the UK Parliament, where several characters in *The Powerhouse* sit as Members."

memes.label: Hyde Park
memes.description: Central London park that serves as a recurring location in the narrative, crossed by Leithen during his flight from the Powerhouse.

memes.label: "Iconoclasm"
memes.description: "Lumley's preferred term for the Powerhouse's mission — the swallowing of formulas — which he distinguishes from mere destruction."

memes.label: "Jenkinson"
memes.description: "An art critic and Old Wedgwood connoisseur who unwittingly provides Leithen with a crucial lead to Andrew Lumley's Blackheath property."

memes.label: "Jermyn Street"
memes.description: "A fashionable West End street where Chapman's over-enthusiasm for detection lands him in trouble in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Julius Pavia
memes.description: A retired East India merchant with whom Charles Pitt-Heron conducted financial operations tied to the Powerhouse.

memes.label: "Knowledge and Will"
memes.description: "The Nietzschean union Lumley seeks — where those who possess secret knowledge join forces with those who have the will to use it."

memes.label: Knowledge as Power
memes.description: The instrumental principle underlying the Powerhouse's threat — that knowledge, wielded without moral constraint by extra-social intelligences, is the only force capable of undermining civilisation

memes.label: "Konalevsky"
memes.description: "A clerk at the Russian Embassy in London who provides Tommy Deloraine with key information about Charles Pitt-Heron's forged passport and his intended destination of Moscow."

memes.label: "Labour Party"
memes.description: The political party representing organised labour in Edwardian Britain, represented in The Powerhouse by the MP Chapman — a man whose bluff independence and suspicion of Capitalism make him a natural, if volatile, ally against Lumley's conspiracy

relations.label: Chapter V: I Take a Partner
Chapter IX: Return of the Wild Geese

memes.label: "Chancery Lane Station"
memes.description: "The London Underground station where Leithen proves he is being followed in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Langham Place"
memes.description: "A short street near Oxford Circus, part of the Bloomsbury area where Macgillivray arranges a fateful dinner in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Law
memes.description: The legal order that the protagonist Sir Edward Leithen embodies as a barrister — both civilisation's primary defence against chaos and, as Lumley argues, a fragile construct that depends on consensus rather than force

memes.label: Leithen's Siege
memes.description: The climactic sequence in Chapter VII during which Leithen's London flat is placed under siege by Powerhouse operatives, his telephone is cut, Chapman is intercepted, and Leithen must engineer a desperate escape to deliver his evidence.

memes.label: "Leithen's Car Breakdown"
memes.description: "The breakdown of Leithen's newly acquired motorcar in rural Surrey at Whitsuntide, a seeming accident that leads to his fateful overnight stay at Andrew Lumley's country house."

memes.label: "Leithen's Flat"
memes.description: "Sir Edward Leithen's flat in Down Street, Mayfair — the scene of the siege and climactic confrontation with Lumley's agents in Chapter IX."

memes.label: Mr. Levison
memes.description: A shopkeeper and artist who assists Leithen in his escape through a back door in The Powerhouse.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The primary setting of *The Powerhouse* — Edwardian London as both civilisation's physical embodiment and the terrain where its fragility is exposed, transforming through the novel from familiar sanctuary to menacing hunting ground

memes.label: "Lord Houghton"
memes.description: "A historical figure cited by the popular press as a comparison for Andrew Lumley in the obituaries following his death, representing the cultivated aristocratic amateur."

memes.label: "Lord Morecambe"
memes.description: "A titled guest at the political dinner in the House of Lords where Leithen observes Andrew Lumley at close quarters, his decorations and eager attention symbolising Lumley's success in penetrating the highest circles of British society."

memes.label: Loyalty
memes.description: Personal loyalty as a binding force among allies, most notably Chapman's loyalty to Tommy Deloraine that overrides his political convictions in *The Powerhouse*

memes.label: "Lumley's Death"
memes.description: "The sudden death of Andrew Lumley from heart failure, which effectively dismantles the Powerhouse conspiracy."

memes.label: "Lutrin"
memes.description: "An art dealer and agent who acts as intermediary between Andrew Lumley and the art market, purchasing the Wentworth Wedgwood plaques on Lumley's behalf."

memes.label: Macgillivray
memes.description: Leithen's contact at Scotland Yard — a professional policeman who provides institutional knowledge about the Powerhouse while remaining bound by the limits of official procedure

memes.label: "Marble Arch"
memes.description: "Marble Arch -- the London landmark and Tube station where Leithen executes a counter-surveillance manoeuvre in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Marylebone Road"
memes.description: "A major east-west road in northwest London, at whose junction with Edgware Road Leithen makes his escape in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Member of Parliament"
memes.description: "The elected representatives in the British House of Commons, a status held by both Leithen and Tommy Deloraine, which plays a significant role in establishing their social position and motivations."
aliases: Member of the House of Commons

memes.label: Midsummer Night
memes.description: Chapter III of *The Powerhouse*, titled "Tells of a Midsummer Night" — the chapter in which Leithen attends Andrew Lumley's dinner party and first hears the philosophy of the Powerhouse articulated

memes.label: "Monsieur Alphonse"
memes.description: "The French chef at the Russian Embassy in London, a temperamental artist of the kitchen whose casserole dish and wounded professional pride feature in Leithen's dramatic arrival at the Embassy."

memes.label: Moscow
memes.description: Russian capital and stated destination of Charles Pitt-Heron's mysterious flight, where Tommy Deloraine's three-week search proves fruitless, serving as the gateway to the novel's Central Asian geography.

memes.label: "My Bank"
memes.description: "\"My Bank\" -- Andrew Lumley's reference to the Bank of England in his philosophical discourse in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Napoleon
memes.description: The historical figure Napoleon Bonaparte, invoked by Andrew Lumley as the benchmark of the kind of absolute, hidden power he claims to wield in *The Powerhouse*

memes.label: "Nihilism"
memes.description: "The form of political extremism that Lumley dismisses as foolish and unsophisticated, contrasted with the Powerhouse's superior methods."

memes.label: "Old Wedgwood"
memes.description: "The area of collecting that leads Leithen to discover Lumley's connection to Pavia in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Order
memes.description: The social and political order that civilisation's compact sustains and that the Powerhouse threatens — not justice or freedom but the mere fact of predictability, the taken-for-granted stability of modern life

memes.label: Organizations
memes.description: Index of all organizations referenced in The Powerhouse

memes.label: "Oxford Circus"
memes.description: "The busy West End junction where Leithen narrowly escapes abduction on his walk home with Chapman in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Oxford Street"
memes.description: "London's premier shopping thoroughfare, where Leithen senses hidden watchers among the crowds in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Park Lane"
memes.description: "Park Lane -- the prestigious Mayfair thoroughfare used by Leithen as an escape route and observation point in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Parliament"
memes.description: The supreme legislative body of the United Kingdom, serving in The Powerhouse as the institutional backdrop against which the characters' secret adventures unfold

memes.label: "Parliament Square"
memes.description: "The square fronting the Houses of Parliament, described as a green and cheerful contrast to the dull interior of the Commons in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Piccadilly
memes.description: Major London thoroughfare running through Mayfair and St James's, setting for Leithen's surveillance by the Powerhouse and his flight through the city.

memes.label: "Piccadilly Circus"
memes.description: "The famous West End junction where Leithen observes that everyone eventually turns up in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Pitt-Heron's Disappearance"
memes.description: The inciting event of the novel — Charles Pitt-Heron's sudden, unexplained flight from London, abandoning his wife Ethel and drawing Sir Edward Leithen into the orbit of the Powerhouse conspiracy

memes.label: Places
memes.description: Index of all locations featured in The Powerhouse, both real and fictional

memes.label: Portman Square
memes.description: London residence of Charles and Ethel Pitt-Heron, a key location in the Powerhouse conspiracy.

memes.label: "Power"
memes.description: "The central theme of John Buchan's *The Powerhouse* — power as knowledge, organization, and the machine of civilization."

memes.label: Profession
memes.description: Professional identity as both armour and constraint — Leithen the barrister, Macgillivray the policeman, each defined by their work and limited by its assumptions

memes.label: "Rat Lane"
memes.description: "Rat Lane -- a rough London street invoked by Bill Docker when describing the terror inspired by the Powerhouse's agents in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Regent Street"
memes.description: "The grand West End thoroughfare, where Chapman keeps futile watch for Lumley and Leithen walks towards danger in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Routh
memes.description: A solicitor consulted by Leithen who provides intelligence on the Powerhouse; formerly a butler named Tuke in the Pitt-Heron household.

memes.label: Saronov
memes.description: A Russian operative within Andrew Lumley's Powerhouse network, dispatched alongside Tuke to intercept Charles Pitt-Heron on his flight through Central Asia.

memes.label: Scotland Yard
memes.description: The headquarters of London's Metropolitan Police — the institutional embodiment of law enforcement whose competence and limitations both illuminate the novel's thesis about civilisation's vulnerability to "extra-social intelligences"

memes.label: Secrecy
memes.description: The operational principle of the Powerhouse and a key narrative driver — secrecy as a weapon that allows a hidden organisation to operate within and against civilisation

memes.label: Secret Society
memes.description: The nature and structure of Andrew Lumley's Powerhouse as a secret society — an international clandestine organisation devoted to the destruction of civilisation from within

memes.label: "Sir Francis Lloyd"
memes.description: "A real historical figure, Major-General Sir Francis Lloyd, K.C.B., to whom John Buchan dedicated The Powerhouse in a prefatory letter explaining the novel's origins as entertainment for soldiers at the front."

memes.label: "South American Interests"
memes.description: "The recurring motif of South American connections in The Powerhouse, linking Lumley's warnings about national fragility, Pitt-Heron's financial dealings, and the Chilean Arbitration."

memes.label: "South London"
memes.description: "The area south of the Thames where Leithen encounters the first clues to the conspiracy in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Stagg
memes.description: A sombre, silent chauffeur employed by the Powerhouse who drives Leithen and later becomes a reluctant ally.

memes.label: "Stock Exchange"
memes.description: London's financial market, invoked in The Powerhouse as part of Andrew Lumley's critique of civilisation's vulnerability — a key component of the economic machinery he believes can be shattered by a single calculated shock

memes.label: "Stratton Street"
memes.description: "A Piccadilly side street where Leithen tests whether he is being followed in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Super-Anarchy
memes.description: Andrew Lumley's term for a new form of anarchism that has learned from civilisation — disciplined, organised, international, and wielded by "extra-social intelligences" rather than illiterate bandits

memes.label: "The Admiralty"
memes.description: The government department responsible for the command of the Royal Navy, appearing in The Powerhouse in the context of a German spy case that provides Leithen with his first "cross-bearing" on Andrew Lumley's network

memes.label: "The Advertising Member"
memes.description: "The unnamed MP in the opening scene of *The Powerhouse* who embodies the tedium of parliamentary routine."

memes.label: The Albany
memes.description: Andrew Lumley's London residence — a rococo Georgian apartment complex in Piccadilly that serves as the nerve center of the Powerhouse conspiracy.

memes.label: "The Amateur"
memes.description: "The Edwardian ideal of the amateur gentleman — embodied by Leithen as a barrister and MP who investigates as a private citizen rather than a professional detective."

memes.label: "The Athenaeum"
memes.description: One of London's most distinguished gentlemen's clubs, cited in The Powerhouse's Admiralty papers as one of Andrew Lumley's club memberships — a marker of his impeccable Establishment credentials

memes.label: "The Bar"
memes.description: "Leithen's profession as a barrister — the foundation of his methodical, evidence-based approach to investigating the Powerhouse."

memes.label: "The Blue Ribbon"
memes.description: "Lord Morecambe's Order of the Garter ribbon, observed by Leithen during the dinner party — a symbol of establishment honours and the civilised order."

memes.label: "The Cabinet"
memes.description: The senior decision-making body of the British Government, present in The Powerhouse as a symbol of the established order whose members unwittingly socialise with the novel's arch-conspirator

memes.label: The Car Chase
memes.description: A motor accident during Leithen's pursuit of Charles Pitt-Heron, when the chauffeur Stagg is forced off the road by a heavy carrier's cart, revealing the Powerhouse's reach.

memes.label: "The Carlton Club"
memes.description: "An exclusive Conservative gentlemen's club in London, whose membership reflects the social standing of Andrew Lumley."

memes.label: "The Chilean Arbitration"
memes.description: "The international arbitration case concerning the Chilean frontier in which Leithen held a junior brief for the British Government, providing the professional backdrop to the novel's framing narrative."

memes.label: "The Chippendale Chairs"
memes.description: "Fine eighteenth-century chairs by Thomas Chippendale that are part of Andrew Lumley's collection at the White Lodge, symbolizing the facade of cultured respectability behind which the Powerhouse operates."

memes.label: The Compact
memes.description: The unspoken social agreement that underpins civilisation — a "silent compact of comfortable folk to keep up pretences" — and the "counter-compact" that threatens to replace it

memes.label: The Confrontation at the Powerhouse
memes.description: The climactic scene of the novel — Leithen's direct verbal confrontation with Andrew Lumley in his Albany chambers, in which Leithen deploys evidence of conspiracy to murder and a psychological portrait of his antagonist to force Lumley's capitulation

memes.label: "The Continental Express"
memes.description: "The train service from Victoria Station that bookends the novel's plot — first carrying Tommy Deloraine into his pursuit eastward, then offering Andrew Lumley his escape route in the final confrontation."

memes.label: "The County Court"
memes.description: "The lower civil court where Edward Leithen first encounters the butler Tuke, a key agent of the Powerhouse."

memes.label: "The County Court Case"
memes.description: "The County Court case on 21 May, a minor taxicab accident suit in South London that brings Leithen into first contact with the Lumley conspiracy through the butler Tuke."

memes.label: "The Dinner at the Albany"
memes.description: "The climactic dinner and confrontation between Leithen and Andrew Lumley at Lumley's Albany chambers, where Leithen presents his evidence and Lumley faces the collapse of his conspiracy."

memes.label: "The Down Street Escape"
memes.description: "Leithen's escape from his Down Street flat after the Powerhouse cuts his communications, a tense departure under surveillance that begins the final round of his confrontation with Lumley."

memes.label: "The Embankment"
memes.description: "The Thames Embankment in London — Leithen's walking route that frames key moments of reflection and danger throughout the novel."

memes.label: The Enemy
memes.description: The thematic treatment of enmity in *The Powerhouse* — from the hunted man's omnipotent terror to the cold professional duel between Leithen and Lumley

memes.label: "The Flat in Down Street"
memes.description: "Sir Edward Leithen's flat in Down Street, Mayfair — his residence, base of operations, and the scene of the climactic siege in Chapter IX."

memes.label: "The Foreign Office"
memes.description: "The British government department responsible for foreign affairs — referenced in the novel's diplomatic context surrounding the Russian Embassy and international intrigue."

memes.label: "The General Staff"
memes.description: "The military metaphor used by Leithen to describe Lumley's operational command structure, comparing the Powerhouse's intelligence network to an army's general staff."

memes.label: The Krafthaus
memes.description: The German word *Krafthaus* ("powerhouse") — the European intelligence term for the secret organisation whose headquarters Andrew Lumley directs, and the original name from which the novel's title derives

memes.label: "The Legion of Honour"
memes.description: "The French order of merit worn by Andrew Lumley at dinner, part of his carefully constructed facade of respectability."

memes.label: The Machine
memes.description: Andrew Lumley's metaphor for modern civilisation — an intricate mechanical system that is powerful but vulnerable, and that can be captured by those who understand its workings

memes.label: The Powerhouse
memes.description: The novel's central metaphor and its organising conspiracy — a vision of "super-anarchy" in which "extra-social intelligences" who have rejected civilisation's "compact" wield knowledge as power to create a disciplined "counter-compact" capable of undermining the established order

memes.label: The Powerhouse (Organization)
memes.description: The secret international conspiracy at the heart of the novel — a network of "extra-social intelligences" led by Andrew Lumley, organised as a "counter-compact" that mirrors and subverts civilisation's institutions

memes.label: The Restaurant in Antioch Street
memes.description: A crucial meeting at Rapaccini's restaurant in Antioch Street where Macgillivray reveals vital intelligence about the Powerhouse conspiracy to Leithen.

memes.label: "Return of the Wild Geese"
memes.description: "The title of Chapter IX, the novel's resolution — marking the safe return of Tommy Deloraine and Charles Pitt-Heron from their pursuit in Central Asia."

memes.label: "The Romanovs"
memes.description: "The Russian imperial dynasty, cited by Lumley as an example of how established governments cooperate to suppress revolutionary threats."

memes.label: The Russian Embassy
memes.description: Diplomatic mission in Belgrave Square that serves as Leithen's sanctuary during his flight from Lumley's agents, representing the only barrier Lumley's network cannot breach.

memes.label: "The Speaker"
memes.description: "The presiding officer of the House of Commons, representing parliamentary order in *The Powerhouse*."
aliases: Speaker of the House of Commons

memes.label: "The Super-Butler"
memes.description: "The concept of a servant whose intelligence far exceeds his station, represented in The Powerhouse by Tuke, Lumley's unnervingly capable butler who is actually a former trade union leader turned criminal agent."

memes.label: "The Super-Butler Trail"
memes.description: "The investigation in Chapter IV where Leithen tracks the movements of Tuke, the super-butler, across London to uncover the Powerhouse network."
aliases: I Follow the Trail of the Super-Butler

memes.label: The Temple
memes.description: Historic legal district in central London housing Sir Edward Leithen's barrister's chambers, his professional base throughout the narrative.

memes.label: "The Times"
memes.description: London's newspaper of record in the Edwardian era, invoked in The Powerhouse as the voice of Establishment opinion and a vector of institutional authority

relations.label: Chapter IX: Return of the Wild Geese

memes.label: "The Treasury"
memes.description: The United Kingdom's finance ministry, appearing in The Powerhouse through the figure of "old Milson" — a civil servant whose presence at Tommy's dinner table symbolises the institutional Establishment that Lumley's conspiracy threatens to undermine

memes.label: "The Wedgwood Examination"
memes.description: "Leithen's visit to the White Lodge at Blackheath with art critic Jenkinson to examine Lumley's Wedgwood plaques, during which Leithen discovers a crucial coded telegram."

memes.label: "The Wedgwood Plaques Discovery"
memes.description: "The discovery in a Piccadilly antique shop that Andrew Lumley had recently purchased a set of Wedgwood plaques, prompting Leithen and Jenkinson's investigation at the White Lodge."

memes.label: "The Wild Goose Chase"
memes.description: "The metaphor for the apparently futile but ultimately essential search for the vanished Charles Pitt-Heron, which structures the early action of The Powerhouse."

memes.label: "The Will to Power"
memes.description: "The Nietzschean concept underlying Lumley's worldview, introduced through a German professor Leithen meets in the Tyrol — the marriage of knowledge and will."

memes.label: Tommy Deloraine
memes.description: Leithen's closest ally in the investigation — a Member of Parliament of "incurable romanticism" whose appetite for adventure makes him a useful but unpredictable partner, serving as the novel's comic-adventurer counterpoint to Leithen's professional caution

memes.label: "Tommy's Journey to Bokhara"
memes.description: "Tommy Deloraine's initial journey from London to Bokhara in search of the vanished Charles Pitt-Heron, traveling via Moscow and the Transcaspian railway."

memes.label: "Tommy's Pursuit to Bokhara"
memes.description: "Tommy Deloraine's race from Bokhara into the Pamir Mountains to overtake Charles Pitt-Heron before Lumley's agents can reach him."

memes.label: "Tottenham Court Road"
memes.description: "A major Bloomsbury thoroughfare marking the eastern boundary of the district containing the trap restaurant in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Trade Union"
memes.description: Organised labour associations in Edwardian Britain, invoked in The Powerhouse as the institutional context for the criminal career of Josiah Routh, a disgraced union official recruited into Lumley's conspiracy

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: The epistemological goal of Leithen's investigation — the truth about the Powerhouse, about Pitt-Heron, about Lumley — and the difficulty of establishing truth in a world of deliberate concealment

memes.label: Tuke
memes.description: Butler to Julius Pavia and operative in Andrew Lumley's Powerhouse network, whose distinctive appearance and unflappable courtesy make him one of Leithen's early clues in tracing the conspiracy.

memes.label: "Victoria Station"
memes.description: "The London railway terminus from which Tommy Deloraine departs on his journey to find Pitt-Heron in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Wedgwood Plaques"
memes.description: "A set of Wedgwood jasperware plaques purchased by Andrew Lumley through agents, which serve as the narrative device leading Leithen to investigate the White Lodge at Blackheath."

memes.label: "West Ham"
memes.description: "West Ham -- the east London district whose drainage scheme forms the subject of Leithen's unglamorous legal brief in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: "Westminster Bridge"
memes.description: "The bridge over the Thames where Leithen watches the Continental express and wonders about Lumley's fate in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: White Lodge
memes.description: Suburban mansion of Julius Pavia, filled with art treasures and linked to the Powerhouse conspiracy.

memes.label: "Whitehall"
memes.description: "The government district of London, home to British ministries and departments, in *The Powerhouse*."

memes.label: Wild Geese
memes.description: The title metaphor of the novel's final chapter, representing the cyclical return of characters from their far-flung adventures.

relations.label: Chapter IX: Return of the Wild Geese

memes.label: "Mr. Wright Davies"
memes.description: "An American gentleman whom Tuke, the butler and Powerhouse operative, claimed to be meeting at Victoria Station on the morning of the taxicab accident -- a story that provides Leithen an early, significant connection between Tommy Deloraine's departure and the Pavia household."

memes.label: Yorkshire
memes.description: Northern English county, home region of Labour MP Chapman and the Deloraine family seat, representing provincial roots in the novel's London-centred narrative.
